posts.title: Free Money for School Is Real — Here's Exactly How to Get It
posts.excerpt: Billions of dollars in grant money go unclaimed every single year. The students who graduate debt-free aren’t luckier than you — they just knew where to look.
posts.author: Student Success Academy
posts.body:
Let's be direct: college is expensive. Tuition, housing, textbooks, fees — the cost adds up fast. But grant money is real, and a lot of it goes unclaimed every year.

💡
Grants are not loans.

That means qualified students do not pay them back. The goal is to know where to look, apply early, and stay organized.

Start with the FAFSA, then check state grants, school aid, and private grant directories. A simple system beats guessing.

Start With the FAFSA

Federal aid is the first place most students should start. Fill out the FAFSA as early as possible, because some aid is limited and deadlines matter.

Check State and School Aid Too

Many students stop after one application. That is a mistake. Your state and your school may each have separate aid programs that can save you thousands.

Quick tip: Search your state name plus the words college grant program and visit the official education website.

Stay Organized

Keep a list of deadlines, required forms, essays, and contacts. Students who stay organized usually win more funding than students who wait until the last minute.

posts.title: Migrating to Linear 101
posts.excerpt: Linear helps streamline software projects, sprints, tasks, and bug tracking. Here's how to get started.
posts.author: Jonathan Wills
posts.body:
Linear is a project management tool built for modern software teams. This post covers everything you need to know about migrating from your current tool to Linear.

Getting started is simple: import your existing issues, set up your cycles, and invite your team.

posts.title: Building your API Stack
posts.excerpt: The rise of RESTful APIs has been met by a rise in tools for creating, testing, and managing them.
posts.author: Lana Steiner
posts.body:
APIs are the backbone of modern web applications. Whether you're building a simple web app or a complex microservices architecture, having a solid API strategy is critical.

In this post, we walk through the tools and techniques that help you build a robust API stack from scratch.

posts.title: Bill Walsh Leadership Lessons
posts.excerpt: Like to know the secrets of transforming a 2-14 team into a 3x Super Bowl winning dynasty?
posts.author: Eve Wilkins
posts.body: Bill Walsh believed that if you set the highest standards in every area of operation, the results would follow. Focus on the process, not the outcome. Build systems, train relentlessly, and trust your team.
